prompt:
Ticket for the analytics sprint: the editor dashboard is getting a "top words" widget and the wordfreq package needs to grow two things to back it.

1) TopK(k) on Counter, returning a []Entry (word + count) of the k most frequent words, most frequent first. Ties MUST break alphabetically — the widget snapshots its output and diffs it between runs, so map-order randomness leaking into the result is an instant bug report. k bigger than the vocabulary just returns everything; k <= 0 returns nothing.

2) Stopword filtering, but at REPORT time, not ingest time. SetStopwords takes a list; those words disappear from TopK output (the next-ranked words fill the slots) but the raw tally — Count, Total, Distinct — must not change, because the same Counter also feeds the vocabulary-size job. Matching is case-insensitive, and each SetStopwords call replaces the previous set outright (passing nil clears it), it does not merge.

topk_test.go is the acceptance contract for all of this. wordfreq_test.go pins today's tokenizing/counting behavior and every bit of it has to keep passing — don't touch either test file. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... is the check.
